652	\section{Positive part of a Weil divisor}
653	% NOTE (iter-190 plan-phase): added to host the Pin 2 corrective per
654	% `task_results/AlgebraicJacobian_RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.lean.md`
655	% (iter-189). `Hom.poleDivisor` is being refactored to return the
656	% positive part of `principal (φ^* t_∞)` so that
657	% `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` is mathematically correct (the principal
658	% divisor has degree 0 by `thm:principal_deg_zero`; only its
659	% positive part can have positive degree).
660	\begin{definition}
661	
662	
663	\leanok
664	[Positive part of a Weil divisor]
665	  \label{def:WeilDivisor_positivePart}
666	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart}
667	  \textit{Source: Project-bespoke (Hartshorne II.6.10 phrasing).}
668	  Let \(X\) be a scheme satisfying the codim-1 hypotheses of
669	  \ref{def:codim1_cycles}. For a Weil divisor
670	  \(D = \sum_Y n_Y \cdot Y \in \mathrm{Div}(X)\), the \emph{positive part}
671	  \((D)_0 \in \mathrm{Div}(X)\) is the Weil divisor
672	  \[
673	    (D)_0 \;:=\; \sum_{Y \,:\, n_Y > 0} n_Y \cdot Y.
674	  \]
675	  Concretely, \((D)_0\) is obtained from \(D\) by replacing each coefficient
676	  \(n_Y\) with \(\max(n_Y, 0)\). Equivalently, in the \(\Finsupp{\mathrm{PrimeDivisor}(X)}{\mathbb Z}\)
677	  presentation, the positive part is obtained either by the pointwise lattice
678	  join with the zero divisor — \(\,(D)_0 = D \sqcup 0\,\) — or, equivalently
679	  and more transparently for typeclass synthesis, by mapping the coefficients
680	  through the zero-preserving function \(n \mapsto n \sqcup 0\):
681	  \(\,(D)_0 = \mathtt{Finsupp.mapRange}\;(n \mapsto n \sqcup 0)\;D\). The two
682	  forms agree by the universal property of \(\mathtt{Finsupp.mapRange}\). The
683	  Lean implementation uses the \(\mathtt{mapRange}\) form to avoid the
684	  noncomputable \(\Finsupp.\mathtt{semilatticeSup}\) synthesis. The complementary \emph{negative part}
685	  \((D)_\infty\) is defined by \((D)_\infty := (-D)_0\), so that
686	  \(D = (D)_0 - (D)_\infty\) is the canonical decomposition into an
687	  effective (non-negative-coefficient) divisor minus an effective divisor.
688	  On a smooth proper curve \(C\) over \(\bar k\), where the prime divisors
689	  are the closed points and the coefficients are integers, the positive part
690	  reduces to keeping only the closed points whose multiplicity is positive
691	  — i.e. precisely the zeros of any rational function whose principal
692	  divisor is \(D\), in the case \(D = \mathrm{div}(f)\).
693	\end{definition}
694	\begin{lemma}
695	
696	\leanok
697	[Degree of the positive part of \(\mathrm{div}(f)\) for a non-constant rational
698	function equals the function-field extension degree]
699	  \label{lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank}
700	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank}
701	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, Proposition~6.9, p.~137 (multiplicativity
702	  % of degree under finite pullback) (read from
703	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 154).
704	  % Stacks pointer: tag 02RW (pullback of divisors under finite
705	  % morphisms), tag 0BE6 (degree under finite morphism).
706	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Proposition 6.9. \emph{Let \(f \colon X \to Y\) be a finite
707	  % morphism of nonsingular curves. Then for any divisor \(D\) on \(Y\),
708	  % \(\deg(f^\ast D) = [K(X) : K(Y)] \cdot \deg D\).}"
709	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, Proposition~6.9, p.~137.}
710	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over an
711	  algebraically closed field \(\bar k\), and let
712	  \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) be a non-constant morphism.
713	  Let \(t_\infty \in K(\mathbb P^1)\) be a local parameter at \(\infty \in \mathbb P^1\)
714	  (equivalently \(t_\infty = 1/u\) for the standard affine coordinate \(u\) on
715	  \(\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}\)). Write \(f := \varphi^{\#} t_\infty
716	  \in K(C)\) for the pullback of \(t_\infty\) along \(\varphi\). Then \(f\) is
717	  nonzero (because \(\varphi\) is non-constant; equivalently \(\varphi^{-1}(\infty)\)
718	  is a proper closed subscheme of \(C\)) and the degree of the positive
719	  part of the principal divisor of \(f\) equals the function-field
720	  extension degree:
721	  \[
722	    \deg\bigl((\mathrm{div}(f))_0\bigr)
723	    \;=\; \bigl[\,K(C) \,:\, \bar k(\mathbb P^1)\,\bigr]
724	    \;=\; \mathrm{Module.finrank}_{\bar k(\mathbb P^1)} K(C).

1140	  preservation step consumed by Step (B) of \cref{lem:gmscaling_chart_PLB_eq}, where the
1141	  chart-\(i\) ring map is post-composed with \(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\) of the relative
1142	  tensor product to identify it with
1143	  $\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \otimes_{\bar k}
1144	  \mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k)$.
1145	\end{lemma}
1146	\begin{lemma}
1147	
1148	\leanok
1149	[Chart-\(1\) \(\mathrm{Proj.appIso}\) evaluation: \(\mathtt{isLocElem} \mapsto [X_0/X_1] \mapsto 1\)]
1150	  \label{lem:iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval}
1151	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval}
1152	  \uses{def:proj_chart_ring_iso, lem:chart_ring_iso_preserves_algebraMap}
1153	  % Archon-original Lean encoding of the chart-\(1\) \(\mathrm{Proj.appIso}\) evaluation step.
1154	  % Extracted in iter-192 (per progress-critic STUCK verdict and blueprint-reviewer MF-1
1155	  % finding: 4 consecutive iters - iter-188/189/190/191 - hit the LOC budget wall on this
1156	  % evaluation inside the body close of \texttt{iotaGm\_chart1\_composition\_isOpenImmersion}).
1157	  % Pulled out as a named standalone hook so the prover can target it directly. The
1158	  % mathematical content is the standard fact that \(\mathrm{Proj.appIso}\) of the chart-\(i\)
1159	  % homogeneous chart map identifies the canonical local section \(\mathtt{isLocElem}\) of
1160	  % \(\Spec(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i)\) with the homogeneous-localisation ratio
1161	  % \([X_{1-i}/X_i] \in \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\). No
1162	  % external % SOURCE QUOTE: this is a Mathlib-side identification + project-side composition.
1163	  Let \(\mathcal A = \bar k[X_0, X_1]\) be the standard \(\mathbb N\)-graded polynomial ring,
1164	  and consider the homogeneous chart morphism at the degree-\(1\) generator \(X_1\),
1165	  \[ \mathrm{Proj.away}\iota\,X_1 \colon \Spec\,(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_1)
1166	     \longrightarrow \Proj\,\mathcal A, \]
1167	  realising the basic open \(D_+(X_1) \subseteq \overline{\mathbb P^1} = \Proj\,\mathcal A\) as
1168	  an affine open. Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Proj.appIso}\) (applied to the open immersion
1169	  \(\mathrm{Proj.away}\iota\,X_1\)) packages, at the level of global sections over \(\top\),
1170	  the canonical scheme-side identification
1171	  \[ \Gamma(\Spec(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_1),\, \top)
1172	     \ =\ \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_1
1173	     \ \xleftarrow{\ \sim\ }\
1174	     \Gamma(\Proj\,\mathcal A,\, D_+(X_1))
1175	     \ =\ \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_1. \]
1176	  Under this identification, the canonical local-section element
1177	  \(\mathtt{isLocElem} \in \Gamma(\Spec(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_1), \top)\)
1178	  (the global section of \(\Spec\) corresponding under the affine Spec / global-sections
1179	  adjunction to the identity ring map) is the image, via
1180	  \((\mathtt{Proj.appIso}\,(\mathrm{Proj.away}\iota\,X_1)).\mathrm{inv}\), of the homogeneous
1181	  fraction
1182	  \[ [X_0/X_1] \ \in\ \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_1 \]
1183	  --- the ratio of the two degree-\(1\) generators, i.e.\ the chart-\(1\) affine coordinate
1184	  \(u = X_0/X_1\). Composing further with the chart-\(1\) ring identification
1185	  \(\mathrm{forward}_1 \colon \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_1 \xrightarrow{\sim}
1186	  \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,\{*\}\,\bar k\) of \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} sends
1187	  \([X_0/X_1]\) to the polynomial generator \(u\); then pre-composing with the source-side
1188	  \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge of \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} (with the trivial
1189	  \(\mathbb G_m\) factor at the identity section \(\lambda = 1\) collapsed by
1190	  \(\mathtt{Algebra.TensorProduct.lid}\)) lands at \(1\) in the codomain ring. The combined
1191	  composite ring map therefore sends \(\mathtt{isLocElem}\) to \(1 \in \bar k\):
1192	  \[ (\mathtt{iotaGm\_chart1\_composition}).\mathrm{appTop}\,(\mathtt{isLocElem})
1193	     \ =\ 1 \quad \text{in the codomain ring.} \]
1194	\end{lemma}
1195	\begin{proof}
1196	  The Mathlib identification of \(\mathrm{Proj.away}\iota\,X_1\) as the open immersion
1197	  presenting the basic open \(D_+(X_1) \subseteq \Proj\,\mathcal A\) packages
1198	  \(\mathtt{Proj.appIso}\,(\mathrm{Proj.away}\iota\,X_1)\) as the canonical iso on global
1199	  sections; its concrete value \(\mathtt{Proj.appIso\_apply}\) traces the canonical local
1200	  section back to the homogeneous-localisation ratio of the two distinguished generators. The
1201	  canonical local-section \(\mathtt{isLocElem}\) is, by the affine Spec / global-sections
1202	  adjunction, the global section of \(\Spec(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_1)\) corresponding to
1203	  the identity ring map; under the inverse identification it becomes the element
1204	  \([X_0/X_1] \in \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_1\), the ratio of the
1205	  two degree-\(1\) generators of \(\mathcal A\). Applying the chart-\(1\) forward map
1206	  \(\mathrm{forward}_1\) of \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} then sends \([X_0/X_1]\) to the
1207	  polynomial-ring generator \(u \in \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\,\{*\}\,\bar k\) (by the defining
1208	  equation \(X_1 \mapsto 1\), \(X_0 \mapsto u\) of the chart-\(1\) iso). Finally, the source-side
1209	  \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge with the Gm-twist collapse is the chart-\(1\) instance
1210	  of the same recipe \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} uses: at the identity section \(\lambda = 1\)
1211	  of \(\mathbb G_m\), the Gm-twist of the chart-\(1\) generator is the chart-\(1\) generator
1212	  itself, and \(\mathtt{Algebra.TensorProduct.lid}\) eliminates the trivial
1213	  \(\mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k\)-factor to send \(u\) to \(1 \in \bar k\). The combined Mathlib

input.new_string:
## Knowledge Base

### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)

- **Prime-avoidance via `Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite` + IH-rewriting on a fresh witness `x'`** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical — Lane G `notMem_minimalPrimes_of_regularLocal_succ` in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`, axiom-clean. Empirically falsifies the iter-191 KB-entry "route (iii) Krull-intersection" sketch — see Known Blockers.)* When closing a `(x) ∈ minimalPrimes R → False` goal inside an induction on `spanFinrank 𝔪 R` with a universally-quantified IH (`hIH : ∀ R' (...) , IsDomain R'`), do NOT fight the specific `x` from the goal. Pick a *fresh* witness `x' ∈ 𝔪 \ (𝔪² ∪ ⋃ minimalPrimes R)` via `Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite`. Then: (1) `R/(x')` is regular local of `spanFinrank = k` via `regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` (iter-190 helper); (2) `IsDomain (R/(x'))` via `hIH`; (3) `(x')` is prime; (4) pick minimal prime `𝔭' ⊆ (x')` via `Ideal.exists_minimalPrimes_le`; `x' ∉ 𝔭'` by avoidance; (5) for `y ∈ 𝔭' ⊆ (x')`: `y = x' · z` with `z ∈ 𝔭'` (primality + `x' ∉ 𝔭'`), so `𝔭' ⊆ x' · 𝔭' ⊆ jacobson R · 𝔭'`; Nakayama (`Submodule.FG.eq_bot_of_le_jacobson_smul`) gives `𝔭' = ⊥`; (6) `⊥ ∈ minimalPrimes R` is prime, so `IsDomain R`; (7) in a domain `(⊥).minimalPrimes = {⊥}` (`Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self`), forcing `(x) = ⊥` ⟹ `x = 0` contradicting `x ∉ 𝔪²`. Key lemmas: `minimalPrimes.finite_of_isNoetherianRing`, `Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite`, `IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal`, `Ideal.minimalPrimes_isPrime`, `Submodule.FG.eq_bot_of_le_jacobson_smul`, `IsLocalRing.ringJacobson_eq_maximalIdeal`, `IsDomain.of_bot_isPrime`. Reusable for any Stacks-00NQ-style "regular local ⟹ domain" closure inside an induction where the IH is universally quantified in the ring.

- **Aliasing-`set y` (NOT `let y`) for instance scope + `change`-to-Y-side-action + key identity via `Scheme.Hom.appLE_appIso_inv`** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical — Lane F `pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict` in `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`, axiom-clean.)* The canonical recipe for restrict-iso-pullback module isomorphism smul-compatibility on `Scheme.Modules`. Step A: `set y : Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤) := ...` (NOT `let y` — `set` substitutes the term in the goal, putting the smul instance on `y`'s type in the `Γ(Spec _, ⊤)`-action defined via the restrict module structure on the carrier; `let` would not propagate the substitution). Step B: `change ((hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv.hom (r • _) = ...)` to re-express the smul as the Y-side action via the rfl-unfold of `restrictFunctor`'s smul. Step C: `rw [Scheme.Modules.map_smul]` to migrate the Y-side scalar through the presheaf restriction. Step D: prove the categorical key identity `(ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ (hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv ≫ Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op = 𝟙`. **Sub-key identity**: `hU.fromSpec.appLE U ⊤ e₀ = (ΓSpecIso Γ(Y, U)).inv` (where `e₀ : ⊤ ≤ hU.fromSpec ⁻¹ᵁ U`) closes via `simp [Scheme.Hom.appLE, hU.fromSpec_app_self, ← Functor.map_comp]` — internally collapses the Spec-side `eqToHom hU.fromSpec_preimage_self ≫ homOfLE e₀ : ⊤ ⟶ ⊤` poset arrow through `Functor.map_id`. ~50 LOC total. Reusable for any restrict-iso-pullback module isomorphism whose smul-compatibility is the residual gap.

- **`private` in Lean 4 controls VISIBILITY, not NAMESPACE REGISTRATION — collisions with imports possible** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical — Lane RRF `H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero` shadow-removal in `RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean`.)* Suppose iter-N landed a `private theorem Foo.bar` as a typed-sorry shadow in file A; iter-(N+1) wants to import `Foo.bar` from file B. Naive expectation: `private` scopes the local declaration so the import resolves to the public version. **Wrong**: `private` controls *visibility* (callability from outside the file) but the fully-qualified name `Foo.bar` still registers in the environment. Lean rejects the redeclaration with `a non-private declaration 'Foo.bar' has already been declared` at the import site, NOT silently shadow. **Remediation**: delete the private placeholder entirely; replace with a `/-! sourcing-change note -/` docstring block. Downstream consumers then resolve to the imported public version automatically. **Alternative**: use a distinctive private name (e.g. `Foo.bar_local`) and add an explicit migration TODO. Reusable for any "land file-local shadow iter-N + public iter-(N+1) consumes downstream" pattern.

- **`HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton` + `Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁` + `Abelian.Ext.addEquiv₀` is the generic abelian-category `Ext^1`-vanishing recipe given SES with injective middle + Hom-surjectivity** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical — Lane H `ext_one_eq_zero_of_hom_surjective_of_injective` in `RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean`, axiom-clean. Substrate for the `i = 1` case of `HModule_flasque_eq_zero`.)* For SES `0 → S.X₁ → S.X₂ → S.X₃ → 0` in an abelian category with `S.X₂` injective and `Hom(X, S.X₂) → Hom(X, S.X₃)` surjective, prove `Ext X S.X₁ 1` is subsingleton via: (1) `HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton` ⟹ `Ext X S.X₂ 1` subsingleton ⟹ `x₁.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.f) = 0` for any `x₁ ∈ Ext X S.X₁ 1`; (2) `Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁` lifts `x₁` to some `x₃ ∈ Ext X S.X₃ 0` via the connecting morphism; (3) `Abelian.Ext.addEquiv₀` identifies `x₃` with a `Hom(X, S.X₃)` — discharge by the surjectivity hypothesis (lift along `S.g`). 230:- **`private` in Lean 4 controls VISIBILITY, not NAMESPACE REGISTRATION — collisions with imports possible** *(NEW iter-192 Archon canonical — Lane RRF `H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero` shadow-removal in `RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean`.)* Suppose iter-N landed a `private theorem Foo.bar` as a typed-sorry shadow in file A; iter-(N+1) wants to import `Foo.bar` from file B. Naive expectation: `private` scopes the local declaration so the import resolves to the public version. **Wrong**: `private` controls *visibility* (callability from outside the file) but the fully-qualified name `Foo.bar` still registers in the environment. Lean rejects the redeclaration with `a non-private declaration 'Foo.bar' has already been declared` at the import site, NOT silently shadow. **Remediation**: delete the private placeholder entirely; replace with a `/-! sourcing-change note -/` docstring block. Downstream consumers then resolve to the imported public version automatically. **Alternative**: use a distinctive private name (e.g. `Foo.bar_local`) and add an explicit migration TODO. Reusable for any "land file-local shadow iter-N + public iter-(N+1) consumes downstream" pattern.
